steps.title: Identify the rule for time expressions
steps.content: In Standard English, durations with ago require plural, non-possessive forms. You say “160 years ago,” not “160 year's ago” or “160 years' ago.”

steps.title: Apply the rule to select the correct forms
steps.content:
D provides plural, non-possessive forms: artists and over 160 years, which correctly creates “over 160 years ago.” Choices A and B violate the rule by adding possessives to the time unit (year's, years'), while C unnecessarily makes artists possessive.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
